topic: Managing MOL filing deadlines across 20 client companies in a shared Excel file — when exactly does it break?
format: Client trust story: the Sanad office that started giving every client a portal to track their own cases — what happened to client retention and referrals in the first three months
post:
One Sanad office in Muscat gave every client company their own portal to track their cases in real time.

No more "what's the status?" calls. No more voice notes at 10pm.

Three months later: zero client churn. Four referrals. Two converted.

That's what happens when clients stop feeling ignored.

---

Here's the exact moment Excel breaks.

Not at 5 clients. Not even 10.

It breaks when an MOL filing slips — not because anyone forgot, but because the cell with the deadline was last touched by someone who left three months ago. And the client finds out before you do.

That's when the trust goes. Not slowly. All at once.

40 work permits, 15 visa renewals, 8 MOL filings across 20 companies — I've seen what that looks like in a shared spreadsheet with no alerts, no audit trail, and no one person who owns it. It's not a workflow. It's a liability waiting to land on a Friday afternoon.

SmartPro Hub was built for the 924 licensed Sanad offices running Oman's government services channel. Automatic expiry alerts. A client portal. MOL filing timelines tracked per company. One place, not twelve tabs.

OMR 12/month to start. 14-day free trial, no card needed.

If you run a Sanad office — how many deadlines are sitting in a file right now that only one person can see? And what happens when that person is on leave?
